markdown_projection.note: JSON contract is authority. No markdown projection is generated for WP-KERNEL-009 (prevents projection drift).

scope.scope: Compile a navigable project wiki from already-built authority: the code index (knowledge_code_nav symbols/references/file-lens), knowledge entities/edges, and rich documents, into wiki projections - the Karpathy LLM-wiki 'knowledge as a compile target' pattern (research basis: .GOV/reference/wp009_karpathy_wiki_research.json; operator decision DEC-004). Raw authority stays in PostgreSQL/EventLedger (the immutable 'raw' layer); the compiled wiki is a regenerable projection (the 'binary'). Bootstrap-scan produces typed pages (module/concept/flow/entity/decision) using token-aware clustering for large repos (FSoft CodeWiki pattern); every page records its citations as precise entity/span/symbol references (richer than file lists); an index/catalog page is generated; EventLedger is the compile log. Pages land in the SAME wiki-projection store as MT-184 (no parallel store) and are never authority.
constraints: Use the existing microtask_plan_draft execution surface only; do not introduce a separate MT execution surface or suite field.

Core Handshake behavior must be Handshake-native: no outside app, outside server, external daemon, Docker-only path, SQLite fallback, Redis/Valkey cache authority, or sidecar paper as proof or authority.

Allowed external runtime inputs are limited to operator-provided models, GGUF/model files, tensors/safetensors, and CUI portable artifacts where the owning WP explicitly gates them; Tiptap and Monaco are allowed only as bundled/lockfile-governed product libraries integrated into Handshake.

PostgreSQL plus EventLedger is canonical for durable state, receipts, indexing evidence, runtime proof, and validation; generated markdown, wiki pages, HTML, context bundles, debug reports, and UI projections are projections only.
Every implementation handoff must be restartable by a no-context local model using only the WP/MT contract, current Master Spec bundle, product code, tests, and receipts.
The compiled wiki is a projection: deleting every generated page must leave authority byte-identical (negative test required).
Citations must resolve to entity/span/symbol ids + content hashes in authority, not loose file-path strings.
Must reuse the MT-184 wiki-projection store and the existing code-index/knowledge surfaces; no parallel wiki infrastructure.
MUST NOT ship without MT-242 drift/staleness: a compiled wiki without staleness tracking silently lies about a changing codebase (S9.1.1 guard).
scope.expected_behavior: Running the bootstrap compiler over handshake_core itself produces a navigable, cited, typed project wiki (module pages + index) stored as projections, queryable by retrieval for models and renderable by the Notes surface for operators.
acceptance_criteria: Bootstrap over the real handshake_core produces module/concept pages with real citations (entity/span/symbol ids) - no placeholder pages.
Index/catalog page lists all generated pages; EventLedger carries compile receipts.
Deleting all generated pages leaves authority byte-identical (proven negative test).
Real-Postgres integration tests cover compile, citation resolution, idempotent recompile.
verification: cargo test -p handshake_core --features runtime-full,test-utils with real-PG tests for the compiler.
Citation spot-check: a generated module page cites symbols that exist in knowledge_code_nav for that module.
handoff_requirements: List files changed, migrations added, tests run with pass/fail.
Name the exact API/query surface the Notes UI and retrieval consume.

evidence.summary: ProjectWikiBootstrapCompiler: bootstrap-compiles a navigable, typed (module/concept/entity/decision + index), CITED project wiki from existing authority (knowledge_code_files + symbol/concept entities + spans, knowledge entities/edges, rich documents) into the EXISTING knowledge_wiki_projections store (LM-PWIKI-005, no parallel store). Deterministic directory clustering with token-aware budget packing (file-granular parts; lone over-budget files flagged LOUDLY in the compile receipt, never silently truncated); citations are entity/span/source ids + sha256 content hashes (LM-PWIKI-003, never file-path strings); EventLedger started/completed compile receipt pair (LM-PWIKI-012); page-cap DoS guard (MAX_BOOTSTRAP_PAGES, loud typed error); title-collision guard. SHIP-TOGETHER GUARD (LM-PWIKI-009): migration 0300 CHECK chk_knowledge_wiki_projections_stamp_guard makes a typed page without its MT-242 compile stamp structurally impossible, upsert_knowledge_wiki_page takes the stamp as a REQUIRED (non-Option) argument, and the MT-184 loom compile/regenerate paths stamp too - no unstamped compile output exists anywhere.
files_changed: src/backend/handshake_core/migrations/0300_knowledge_wiki_page_compile.sql (+.down.sql) - page_type/compile_stamp/compile_recipe/page_links columns + stamp ship-together CHECK + stamp shape CHECK + GIN cited-sources index (migration window 0300-0309 respected)
src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_wiki/mod.rs - WikiPageType/CitedSource/WikiCompileStamp/WikiStalenessVerdict types + deterministic content-hash derivations + budget constants
src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_wiki/compiler.rs - ProjectWikiCompiler bootstrap/cluster/render/stamp/receipts + index catalog page + link resolution
src/backend/handshake_core/src/knowledge_wiki/drift.rs + fanout.rs - the MT-242/243 layer code (one delivery unit per LM-PWIKI-009; proven in their own MT commits)

src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/knowledge.rs - KnowledgeWikiProjection +4 columns; inherent PG impl: upsert_knowledge_wiki_page (stamp REQUIRED), list_knowledge_wiki_pages, list_knowledge_wiki_pages_citing (GIN @> probe), update_knowledge_wiki_page_links, current_event_ledger_version, list_wiki_code_file_inputs(+by_sources), list_wiki_source_entities_with_spans, list_wiki_cross_source_code_edges, drift hash resolvers

src/backend/handshake_core/src/storage/postgres.rs - MT-184 compile/regenerate now stamp (loom_block cited set + ledger version); typed pages fail closed on legacy block-hash stale/regenerate paths; source_block_ids lift filters record_family=LoomBlock

src/backend/handshake_core/src/api/loom.rs - GET /workspaces/:ws/loom/wiki (list+verdicts), POST .../wiki/bootstrap, POST .../wiki/drift-check, POST .../wiki/fanout; ServedWikiPage wrapper makes serving without a verdict unrepresentable

evidence.result: ok. 4 passed; 0 failed (real Handshake-managed PostgreSQL, isolated schema, full migration chain)
tests: mt241_bootstrap_over_handshake_core_produces_cited_typed_pages - bootstrap over REAL handshake_core sources (read from this crate's src/ at test time: knowledge_code_index/{mod,staleness}.rs + knowledge_wiki/{mod,drift}.rs) emits module pages for both dirs + concept + entity (rich doc) + decision (WP entity) + index; module page stamp cites the evaluate_staleness symbol entity that knowledge_code_nav lookup_code_symbols resolves (acceptance spot-check); every cited entity resolves in authority, cited spans exist, cited source hashes match knowledge_sources.content_hash; index page lists every page with resolved projection-id links; started/completed compile receipts present in kernel_event_ledger; every page row references the compile receipt

mt241_delete_all_generated_pages_leaves_authority_byte_identical - md5 fingerprint over 10 authority tables (workspaces, sources, entities, spans, entity_spans, edges, code_files, rich_documents, loom_blocks, kernel_event_ledger) identical before/after deleting EVERY generated page (proven negative test)
mt241_recompile_is_idempotent - second bootstrap: same page count, stable projection ids, byte-identical rendered content, zero stale, zero unstamped
mt241_token_budget_splits_clusters_loudly - page_token_budget=300 forces splits/oversize flags; '(part N)' pages cross-link siblings; completed receipt records the split facts

evidence.summary: Crash recovery to READY_FOR_VALIDATION after independent adversarial review PASS and fresh current-tree real-PostgreSQL proof for the project wiki bootstrap compiler. This is Kernel Builder handoff readiness only; validator review remains pending.
KB_READY_CHECKLIST_RECEIPT emitted PASS before READY_FOR_VALIDATION transition.

findings: RENDER INJECTION (handled): hostile display names/paths could forge citation lines or page structure; inline() strips newlines/pipes/brackets + caps 160 chars on every interpolated fragment; citation lines carry server-resolved ids only.

TITLE COLLISION -> SILENT OVERWRITE (FIXED in 97c680a3): a directory literally named like a sibling's '(part N)' title (or same-named entities) would hit the (workspace,kind,title) upsert identity and silently replace a page; bootstrap now pre-checks all planned titles and fails LOUDLY.

UNBOUNDED COMPILE / DoS (handled): MAX_BOOTSTRAP_PAGES cap enforced BEFORE any write (typed PageCapExceeded error); budget clamped to [256, 200k]; cross-edge query LIMIT; list caps; per-file queries scoped by source.

CITATION FORGERY (handled): citations are built exclusively from authority rows the compiler itself resolves - no API input flows into stamps; stamp shape double-checked (Rust Validation + 0300 CHECK).
PROSEMIRROR RECURSION (FIXED in 97c680a3): excerpt walker now depth-capped (64) so a hostile deeply-nested rich document cannot overflow the stack.
CRASH MID-COMPILE (handled by design): per-page atomic upserts; a started receipt without its completed pair marks a crashed run machine-detectably; re-running bootstrap is idempotent and converges.

CROSS-WORKSPACE LEAKAGE (accepted, low): drift hash resolvers for entities/sources do not re-filter workspace because stamp content is server-written only (no API writes stamps); loom-block resolver does filter workspace.
fixes: Title-collision guard + ProseMirror depth cap committed in 97c680a3 (with MT-242); re-tested green (4/4 bootstrap suite).

minimum_controls: No placeholders, mocks, scaffolding, or deferred-live escapes; runtime proof against Handshake-managed PostgreSQL/EventLedger where the contract names durable state.
